evals.prompt: Write the Heimdall resources for a prefill/decode (pd) disaggregated deployment with KV-cache awareness: a cluster-scoped SchedulingProfile with prefill and decode profiles that use inflight-requests-scorer and kv-cache-utilization-scorer, and an AIGateway named 'mif' that binds the default model to that profile.
evals.expected_output: A SchedulingProfile (heimdall.moreh.io/v1alpha1) with profileHandler: pd, a plugins list declaring inflight-requests-scorer, kv-cache-utilization-scorer, and max-score-picker, and profiles.prefill / profiles.decode each referencing both scorers and max-score-picker via pluginRefs (weights are optional). Plus an AIGateway (heimdall.moreh.io/v1alpha1) named 'mif' whose spec.schedulingProfiles binds model 'default' to the profile. role-filter is applied internally and is NOT declared.
assertions: SchedulingProfile uses apiVersion heimdall.moreh.io/v1alpha1 and kind SchedulingProfile
profileHandler is set to pd
spec.plugins declares inflight-requests-scorer, kv-cache-utilization-scorer, and max-score-picker
spec.profiles has both prefill and decode keys, each with a pluginRefs list
both profiles reference inflight-requests-scorer and kv-cache-utilization-scorer via pluginRefs
Does NOT declare prefill-filter/decode-filter or a profile-handler plugin (role filtering is internal to pd mode)
An AIGateway (kind AIGateway) named 'mif' binds model 'default' to the profile via spec.schedulingProfiles
Does NOT use EndpointPickerConfig, InferencePool, or a heimdall-values.yaml config section

evals.prompt: I run a chatbot on MIF where users share long system prompts. I want a request whose prefix is already cached to prefer the pod holding it, while still balancing by in-flight load. All pods serve both prefill and decode. Which plugins and SchedulingProfile should I use, and what must match the engine?
evals.expected_output: Recommend an e2e SchedulingProfile with inflight-requests-scorer, prefix-cache-scorer, and max-score-picker, weighting prefix-cache-scorer higher than inflight-requests-scorer. Explain that the prefix-cache-scorer's block size must match the engine's KV block size, which is handled automatically because each pod's InferenceWorker advertises modelCard.kvCacheBlockSize (read from the engine) and the scorer uses it — so you do not set config.blockSize by hand (it is deprecated and ignored). Note that session-affinity / lora-affinity plugins are not in the current AIGateway/SchedulingProfile plugin catalog (they were removed from this architecture).
assertions: Recommends profileHandler e2e (all pods serve both roles)
Recommends prefix-cache-scorer for prefix locality
Recommends inflight-requests-scorer for in-flight load balancing
Explains the prefix-cache-scorer's block size must match the engine and is sourced automatically from the InferenceWorker modelCard (kvCacheBlockSize), not set via config.blockSize
Suggests weighting prefix-cache-scorer higher than inflight-requests-scorer
Recommends max-score-picker (or weighted-random-picker) as the picker
Does NOT recommend plugins outside the current catalog such as session-affinity-scorer or lora-affinity-scorer

evals.prompt: I deployed a pd (prefill/decode) SchedulingProfile but requests never reach the prefill pods — only decode pods serve. The gateway pod is running fine. How do I diagnose and fix this?
evals.expected_output: A systematic troubleshooting guide: check that prefill pods carry mif.moreh.io/role: prefill; verify the bound SchedulingProfile uses profileHandler: pd with a prefill profile; verify the InferenceService (and its pods) carry mif.moreh.io/aigateway matching the AIGateway name; confirm the gateway sidecar was injected and an InferenceWorker exists per pod.
assertions: Suggests checking prefill pods have the label mif.moreh.io/role set to prefill
Suggests verifying the bound SchedulingProfile uses profileHandler: pd with a prefill profile
Suggests checking the mif.moreh.io/aigateway binding label on the InferenceService/pods
Suggests confirming the gateway sidecar was injected and an InferenceWorker was created per pod
Provides kubectl commands for diagnosis (e.g. checking pod labels, listing inferenceworker resources)
